key_insight: The paper constructs paired Hermite and ultraspherical/Legendre-based polynomial families that enable high-order, C^m finite element bases for arbitrary even-order differential equations.
engineering_relevance: This gives FEM practitioners a systematic way to build smooth, high-order elements with interior bubble functions, improving approximation quality for solid mechanics and other PDEs with higher spatial derivatives.
method: Builds C^m nodal Hermite splines on [0,1] for any m≥0
Derives complementary interior bubble polynomials from (m+1)th derivatives of Legendre polynomials on [-1,1]
Uses endpoints plus ultraspherical roots to form interpolants and prove interpolation error/superconvergence results
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical error formulas, superconvergence analysis, and computational results on a model problem that match the developed basis construction.
watch_item: The construction is mathematically elegant but specialized to p≥2m+2 and may require careful implementation of high-order basis evaluation and conditioning.
paper_title: Complementary families of approximating polynomials with applications to finite element methods applied to differential equations of arbitrary even spatial order
